commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No repair branch is needed; the remaining action is coordinated maintainer/automerge handling for the accepted v0 slice.; Cleared: No supply-chain or code-execution concern was found; the privacy-sensitive contact export is local, read-only, limited by the PR, and accepted in discussion. (sha=e751fb99b8234e99d27f0c31d71fab7f5e40de44)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `telecrawl [--json] contacts export`, exposes it as a read-only `contact-export` crawlkit metadata command, and tests the two-field contact export JSON contract.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new CLI/control metadata capability rather than a broken existing behavior. The PR body supplies copied after-fix built-binary export and clawdex dry-run output for the feature path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair branch is needed; the remaining action is coordinated maintainer/automerge handling for the accepted v0 slice.

target.title: Expose Telegram contact export command
target.body:
## Summary

- add the human CLI `telecrawl [--json] contacts export`
- advertise the machine command `telecrawl --json contacts export` as read-only `contact-export` in crawlkit metadata
- export stored Telegram contacts with only the v0 contract fields: `display_name` and `phone_numbers`

## Related PRs

- Consumer: https://github.com/openclaw/clawdex/pull/2
- WhatsApp producer: https://github.com/steipete/wacrawl/pull/12
- Telegram producer: https://github.com/openclaw/telecrawl/pull/9

These three PRs are one contact-export v0 slice. Source crawlers own source-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling the crawler metadata `contact-export` command. They should land in lockstep; if the command name, metadata argv, envelope, or field names change in one repo, all three should change together.

## Intent

The discoverable local `contact-export` metadata command is intentional. Local opt-in export of stored contact display names and phone numbers is intentional. This is the feature boundary, not an accidental privacy expansion.

The command is local and read-only. It reads the existing encrypted local telecrawl archive; it does not fetch remote data and does not include messages, raw paths, source ids, usernames, JIDs/LIDs, interaction counts, ranking signals, or graph/candidate fields.

## Boundary

This is the Telegram producer side of the same contact-import slice as `openclaw/clawdex#2` and `steipete/wacrawl#12`. The crawler owns Telegram-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling the metadata-advertised `contact-export` command.

This is intentionally a local CLI/control metadata surface, not a generic crawler-to-crawler protocol, graph layer, or candidate model.

## Validation

Current head: `e751fb99b8234e99d27f0c31d71fab7f5e40de44`.

- `nix shell nixpkgs#go_1_26 -c sh -lc 'GOWORK=off go test -count=1 ./... && GOWORK=off go test -count=1 -race ./... && GOWORK=off go mod verify && GOWORK=off go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@v1.3.0 && GOWORK=off "$(go env GOPATH)/bin/govulncheck" ./... && git diff --check origin/main...HEAD'`
- built-binary export smoke, aggregate only: `{"count":200,"root_keys":["contacts"],"contact_keys":[["display_name","phone_numbers"]]}`
- clawdex dry-run smoke from the built `telecrawl` binary, aggregate actions only: `[{"action":"create","count":147},{"action":"update","count":53}]`
- GitHub checks are green on this head: lint, docker, test, deps, release-check, secrets, and Socket checks
- local ClawSweeper-prompt preflight with sub-agents found no accepted code, test, metadata, proof, or cross-repo contract finding remaining

## Privacy

The export does not include usernames, JIDs, LIDs, message bodies, raw paths, source row ids, or interaction counts. Proof output reports counts and JSON key shape only; no real contact names or phone numbers are included here.

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
target.author: joshp123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
